metadata.description: Canonical failure-mode backbone spanning infection, fixation failure, medical events, and specialty-specific complications.
Cross-cutting surgical and healing complications applicable across orthopaedic domains.
Systemic perioperative medical events that alter orthopaedic outcomes and disposition.
Failure modes after fractures, open injuries, and soft-tissue trauma.
Failure modes after soft-tissue reconstruction and sports procedures.
Arthroplasty-specific infectious, mechanical, and soft-tissue failure modes.
Neural, fusion, and construct-related complications after spine care.
Growth-related and developmental complications unique to skeletally immature patients.
Host, injury, implant, and process factors that increase complication likelihood.
Prophylaxis and process measures that reduce complication incidence.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Early Recognition of Complications

metadata.description: Surveillance patterns and red flags enabling timely diagnosis.
Structured evaluation of suspected orthopaedic and medical complications.
First-line management principles for orthopaedic complications before salvage pathways.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Salvage Options After Complications

metadata.description: Revision, reconstruction, and endpoint options when primary management fails.
Functional, reoperation, and quality-of-life consequences of major complications.
Perioperative and injury-related measures that reduce surgical site and implant infection.
Pharmacologic and mechanical strategies to reduce venous thromboembolism after orthopaedic injury or surgery.
Serial examination and documentation to detect evolving neurovascular compromise.
Techniques that protect skin, muscle, and envelope integrity to reduce wound and infection complications.
Mechanical stability needed to prevent loss of fixation, nonunion, and implant failure.
A fracture communicating with the external environment through a traumatic wound.
Pathologic elevation of compartment pressure threatening tissue perfusion and viability.
Infection involving a prosthetic joint and surrounding tissues.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Aseptic Loosening of THA

metadata.description: Noninfectious loss of fixation of a total hip arthroplasty component.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Aseptic Loosening of TKA

metadata.description: Noninfectious loss of fixation of a total knee arthroplasty component.
Replacement of the femoral head and acetabulum with prosthetic components.
Replacement of the tibiofemoral and often patellofemoral articulations with prosthetic components.
Urgent surgical release of involved osteofascial compartments.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Open Fracture Irrigation and Debridement

metadata.description: Operative removal of contamination and devitalized tissue with irrigation of an open fracture wound.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Revision Total Hip Arthroplasty

metadata.description: Reoperation to exchange one or more THA components.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Revision Total Knee Arthroplasty

metadata.description: Reoperation to exchange one or more TKA components.
Proximal femoral articular head at risk for avascular necrosis.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Femoral Head Blood Supply

metadata.description: Vascular supply of the femoral head including medial femoral circumflex contributions.
Proximal humeral articular head.
Peripheral nerve at risk in humeral shaft injury and lateral approaches.
Peripheral nerve at risk at the wrist and forearm.
Peripheral nerve at risk in acetabular and hip surgery.
Neural structure at risk in spine trauma and surgery.
Growth plate whose injury can produce growth arrest and deformity.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Anterior Compartment of the Leg

metadata.description: Osteofascial compartment commonly involved in acute compartment syndrome.

existingProposals.proposed_entity_label: Deep Posterior Compartment of the Leg
existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Deep Posterior Compartment of the Leg

metadata.description: Deep posterior leg compartment relevant to compartment syndrome and neurovascular risk.
Ball-and-socket joint of the proximal femur and acetabulum.
Distal femoral articular condyles of the knee.
Fibrous capsule enclosing a synovial joint.
Peripheral nerve at risk at the fibular neck and lateral knee.
Shaft of the femur.
Infection occurring in the setting of a fracture and its treatment.
Fracture healing that progresses more slowly than expected.
Failure of a fracture to progress to union without further intervention.
Fracture healing in unacceptable alignment.
Deep venous thrombosis or pulmonary embolism associated with injury and recovery.
Failure to maintain achieved fracture or joint alignment.
Ischemic bone death after disruption of blood supply.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Complex Regional Pain Syndrome

metadata.description: Disproportionate regional pain and autonomic dysfunction after injury or surgery.
Particle-induced bone loss around arthroplasty components.
Embolization of fat to the pulmonary or systemic circulation after long-bone injury or instrumentation.
Irreversible muscle death after prolonged ischemia or compartment syndrome.
Skeletal muscle breakdown releasing myoglobin and risking renal injury.
Surgical or traumatic loss of a limb as a salvage or endpoint of severe injury/complication.
Radial nerve motor or sensory deficit associated with humeral injury or surgery.
Median nerve dysfunction after injury, compression, or surgery.
Ischemic muscle contracture after untreated or delayed compartment syndrome.
Failure of the knee extensor mechanism after trauma or arthroplasty.
Loss of fixation of a femoral arthroplasty stem.
Degenerative ankle joint disease after fracture or ligament injury.
Avascular necrosis of the talus after talar neck or body injury.
Avascular necrosis of the humeral head after proximal humerus injury or treatment.
Infection of the surgical wound or deep tissues after an orthopaedic procedure.
Delayed healing, dehiscence, drainage, necrosis, or other wound-healing failure.
Mechanical failure of an implant construct including breakage, bending, or disengagement.
Failure of an implant-bone interface to maintain intended stability.
Loss of implant fixation with motion at the bone-implant or cement interface.
Fracture or fatigue failure of an implant component.
Painful or functionally bothersome prominence of hardware under soft tissues.
Excessive periarticular fibrosis producing joint stiffness and restricted motion.
Ectopic bone formation in soft tissues after trauma or surgery.
Iatrogenic or traumatic injury to nerves or vessels associated with orthopaedic injury or care.
Pain persisting beyond expected recovery and impairing function.
Return to the operating room related to the index injury, procedure, or complication.
Reoperation to revise fixation, reconstruction, or arthroplasty after failure or complication.
Thrombosis of deep veins, commonly of the lower extremity, after orthopaedic injury or surgery.
Embolization of thrombus to the pulmonary arterial tree.
Lower respiratory infection in the perioperative period.
Infection of the urinary tract, often associated with catheterization or immobility.
Acute fluctuating disturbance of attention and cognition after surgery.
Acute decline in renal function in the perioperative or post-injury period.
Perioperative myocardial ischemia, arrhythmia, heart failure, or related cardiac event.
Unplanned return to hospital after discharge related to complications or medical events.
Death related to injury, surgery, or perioperative medical events.
Degenerative joint disease after articular injury or malreduction.
Unacceptable angular, translational, or length deformity after fracture care.
Loss of limb length after fracture, growth disturbance, or reconstruction.
Unacceptable rotational malalignment after fracture healing or fixation.
Restricted range of motion after injury or sports surgery.
Return of joint instability after repair or reconstruction.
Structural or functional failure of a reconstruction graft.
Recurrent tear of a repaired or reconstructed soft-tissue structure.
Pain that fails to resolve after expected sports-injury recovery.
Progression of articular cartilage damage after injury or incomplete treatment.
Dislocation or instability of an arthroplasty construct.
Noninfectious loss of implant fixation around arthroplasty components.
Bearing-surface wear of polyethylene generating debris and secondary osteolysis.
Fracture around an arthroplasty implant.
Disruption or incompetence of the extensor mechanism after knee arthroplasty or trauma.
Incidental or intentional opening of the dura during spine surgery.
New or worsened neurologic deficit after spine injury or surgery.
Degeneration and symptoms at a level adjacent to prior fusion or instrumentation.
Failure of intended spinal fusion.
Failure or kyphosis at the junction of instrumented and non-instrumented spine.
Breakage, pullout, or disengagement of spinal instrumentation.
Cessation of physeal growth after injury, infection, or surgery.
Coronal or sagittal angular deformity from asymmetric growth or malunion.
Inequality of lower extremity length after physeal injury or treatment.
Bony bridge across the physis causing growth disturbance.
Laboratory, imaging, and sampling evaluation for orthopaedic infection.
Clinical assessment and imaging used to diagnose DVT or PE.
Adjunctive invasive pressure measurement when compartment examination is unreliable.
Repeated neurovascular examinations used to detect interval deterioration.
Surgical removal of prominent, failed, or infected hardware.
Reoperation to restore stability after loss of fixation or nonunion.
Operative debridement and irrigation for infection or contaminated wounds.
Staged explant, spacer, and reimplantation pathway for prosthetic joint infection.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Orthopaedic complications form a reusable failure-mode backbone that links prevention, early recognition, workup, management, and salvage across specialties.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Effective complication care starts with risk-factor recognition and prevention rather than reactive management alone.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Infection prevention combines host optimization, soft-tissue protection, timely antibiotics when indicated, and sterile technique.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Suspected surgical site or implant infection requires structured workup before escalating to reoperation or revision.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Open fractures carry elevated risk of fracture-related infection and require early antibiotics, soft-tissue care, and debridement principles.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Nonunion evaluation addresses mechanical stability, biology, infection, host factors, and time course before revision fixation.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Delayed union and nonunion are related but not interchangeable; progression timeline and intervention threshold matter.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Loss of fixation and hardware failure often reflect construct stability mismatch, bone quality, reduction quality, or load-sharing failure.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Preserved distal pulses do not exclude acute compartment syndrome; pain out of proportion and passive stretch pain remain central.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; incidence estimates require source provenance before publication

metadata.claim_text: Venous thromboembolism risk after major orthopaedic injury or arthroplasty is mitigated by risk-stratified prophylaxis and early mobilization when safe.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism are linked VTE events with distinct diagnostic urgency and treatment thresholds.
Prosthetic joint infection management depends on timing, organism, implant stability, and host status; options range from debridement to staged revision.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Aseptic loosening and periprosthetic infection can present similarly; infection must be excluded before revision for loosening.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Polyethylene wear debris can drive osteolysis and secondary implant loosening even when the patient is relatively asymptomatic.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Avascular necrosis risk tracks vascular disruption of the femoral head, talus, or humeral head after injury or treatment.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Arthrofibrosis and joint stiffness require early recognition of motion loss and coordinated therapy before advanced salvage.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Graft failure and re-tear after soft-tissue reconstruction may reflect technical, biologic, rehabilitation, or re-injury factors.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: New or progressive neurologic deficit after spine injury or surgery is time-sensitive and requires immediate evaluation.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Durotomy management prioritizes watertight repair principles, posture/activity guidance, and monitoring for CSF leak sequelae.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Physeal bar formation can produce growth arrest, angular deformity, and leg-length discrepancy in skeletally immature patients.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Complex regional pain syndrome is a diagnosis of disproportionate regional pain with sensory, vasomotor, sudomotor, or motor/trophic features after injury or surgery.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Counsel that reoperation and revision surgery are management-changing endpoints requiring diagnosis of the failure mode before salvage selection.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Hospital readmission after orthopaedic care often clusters around infection, VTE, wound problems, pain control failure, and medical decompensation.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture scope; incidence/threshold claims require provenance and human review

metadata.claim_text: Early recognition of complications depends on serial examination, expected recovery trajectories, and explicit escalation thresholds.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Wound erythema, drainage, fever, or rising inflammatory markers after orthopaedic surgery
metadata.action: Initiate infection workup, hold nonessential antibiotics when cultures are planned, and escalate for possible operative debridement

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Suspected infection after fracture fixation with wound issues, nonunion, or systemic signs
metadata.action: Obtain targeted workup and plan debridement with implant retention versus removal based on stability and timing

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Clinical findings concerning for evolving acute compartment syndrome
metadata.action: Remove constriction, perform serial examination, and escalate for emergent fasciotomy without delaying for imaging alone

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Acute dyspnea, hypoxia, chest pain, or hemodynamic change with VTE risk
metadata.action: Activate urgent medical evaluation and diagnostic pathway for pulmonary embolism while supporting oxygenation and circulation

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: New or progressive motor, sensory, or bowel/bladder deficit after spine injury or surgery
metadata.action: Perform immediate neurologic examination, protect the spine as indicated, and escalate for urgent imaging and specialist intervention

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Loss of pulse, expanding hematoma, progressive neurologic deficit, or threatened limb perfusion
metadata.action: Document status, reduce gross deformity when appropriate, and escalate immediately for vascular or nerve evaluation

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Suspected prosthetic joint infection with pain, effusion, wound drainage, or abnormal labs
metadata.action: Complete infection workup before revision and select DAIR, one-stage, or two-stage strategy with attending input

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Progressive pain, radiographic lucency, or implant migration after infection is excluded
metadata.action: Plan revision arthroplasty strategy based on bone loss, implant track record, and host factors

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Broken implant, loss of alignment, or construct failure on surveillance imaging
metadata.action: Protect the limb, obtain comparison imaging, and plan revision fixation or alternative construct

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Failure of expected clinical or radiographic progression toward union
metadata.action: Evaluate infection, stability, biology, and host factors before selecting revision or biologic augmentation

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Healed fracture with functionally significant malalignment, shortening, or rotation
metadata.action: Quantify deformity and discuss osteotomy, revision, or accept-and-adapt pathways with attending

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Suspected AVN after femoral neck, talar, or proximal humerus injury
metadata.action: Obtain dedicated imaging and counsel joint-preservation versus arthroplasty options by stage and joint

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Early progressive motion loss after fracture care or soft-tissue reconstruction
metadata.action: Intensify supervised therapy, exclude infection or mechanical block, and escalate for lysis/MUA when indicated

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Recurrent instability or imaging evidence of graft failure after ligament reconstruction
metadata.action: Assess tunnel position, graft integrity, alignment, and rehabilitation before revision reconstruction planning

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Asymmetric growth, angular deformity, or LLD after physeal injury
metadata.action: Image the physis, quantify bar and remaining growth, and plan bar resection versus guided growth/osteotomy

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Chest pain, arrhythmia, hypotension, or suspected ACS after orthopaedic surgery
metadata.action: Activate medical emergency response and stabilize while pausing nonessential orthopaedic progression

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Life-threatening decompensation or expected high mortality trajectory after major orthopaedic injury/surgery
metadata.action: Escalate critical care, clarify goals of care, and coordinate multidisciplinary decision-making

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Complications manufacture; management-changing and time-sensitive items require attending review

metadata.trigger: Early post-discharge wound, VTE, infection, or medical warning signs
metadata.action: Expedite evaluation, risk-stratify for ED/readmission, and address root cause before discharge planning
